name: Search existing consignor / exporter
pageTitle: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
caption: Traders
heading: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
secondaryActions: Create a new consignor or exporter
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (search inputs nested in two govuk-grid-column-one-third columns)

structure.text: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
Traders search results table (visually hidden caption)
Columns: Name | Address | Country | View | Select. Rows: e.g. 'ALLIANCE GROUP (NZ) LTD', 'BIOLINE AGROSCIENCES LTD' — each with a View and a Select action button.
Create a new consignor or exporter

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Search for an existing consignor or exporter'
Groups the Name and Address search inputs under a 'Search' legend
Name and Address free-text search filters

The two <label> elements for the Name and Address search inputs are plain govuk-label (no size modifier). Separately, govuk-label--s appears on 6 <h3> elements — the field headings (Name/Address/Country) inside the mobile stacked-card layout, 3 per result row × 2 rows — NOT on the input labels.
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 86 (before) — the two <label> for name/address are class 'govuk-label' with no --s; the 6 govuk-label--s are all H3 headings in the transporter-table-mobile card view

govukComponents.usage: Primary 'Search' button that submits the filter
Search results — columns Name, Address, Country, View, Select; one row per matching existing trader
'Create a new consignor or exporter' — escape hatch to the create-new-trader page when no existing trader matches
Visible horizontal rule separating the search panel from the results
'Traders search results table' caption announced to screen readers only

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The per-row 'View' and 'Select' actions are rendered as <button class="link-button"> — real submit buttons styled to look like links, each posting the chosen trader id back to the server.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System class. Buttons-styled-as-links is a bespoke IPAFFS pattern; behaviour (a POST that mutates the draft notification) is a button, but the visual is a link, which muddies the affordance.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Either a govuk-button--secondary per row, or standard govuk-link anchors inside the table cells if the action can be a GET. The new app should pick one honest affordance per action.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 86 (before) — the DOM contains 8x BUTTON.link-button: View/Select per row for 2 rows, duplicated across the desktop table and the mobile stacked-card layout (4 per layout)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke responsive-table widget. It renders the results twice — a desktop <table> (traders-search-results-form-desktop) AND a mobile stacked-card view (traders-search-results-form-mobile / transporter-table-mobile) where each result becomes a series of H3.govuk-label--s + P pairs (Name / Address / Country). CSS shows/hides one or the other by viewport.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System component. It duplicates the results markup for two layouts, is IPAFFS-specific, and re-uses a 'transporter-table-mobile' class here on a traders table (copy-paste smell). It also introduces H3 headings inside table data purely for the mobile card layout.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Table is already responsive-friendly with sensible column widths; if card-stacking on mobile is genuinely needed the new app can use a single responsive table or the community 'responsive table' pattern rather than emitting two parallel DOM trees.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom container/layout classes wrapping the search fieldset and the results cells.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS-specific styling hooks, not Design System utilities. Cosmetic but each is a bespoke CSS dependency the rebuild would otherwise inherit.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-grid-row / govuk-grid-column-* and govuk-!-* spacing utilities cover the layout without bespoke class names.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A non-namespaced 'form-group' class appears alongside the standard 'govuk-form-group'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bare 'form-group' collides conceptually with Bootstrap-style naming and is not a GOV.UK class; likely legacy markup.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-form-group (already present) is the standard wrapper.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.conditionalOn: Optional search filter — narrows the existing-trader results by trader name. Free-text, no enum/options.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 86 (before) — controls.txt: INPUT|text|name|name|false. NOT driven by any test: the page object SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts (page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts:1-9) exposes ONLY linkCreateNewConsignorOrExporter — no locator for this input — so required=false and the absence of validation are from rendered reality alone, never asserted.

fields.name: address
fields.label: Address
fields.conditionalOn: Optional search filter — narrows the existing-trader results by address. Free-text, no enum/options.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 86 (before) — controls.txt: INPUT|text|address|address|false. NOT driven by any test (see 'name' field): the page object exposes no locator for this input.

corroboration.source2: ipaffs-qa-automation (QA tests only — IPAFFS app source not consulted, out of scope)
corroboration.reverified: All source-2 citations re-checked against the live ipaffs-qa-automation checkout at this corroboration pass. Confirmed exact: page object SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts is a 9-line file exposing ONLY linkCreateNewConsignorOrExporter (getByRole('link', { name: 'Create a new consignor or' }) — a truncated/partial name match); the create-new-only click lands at ched-p-workflows.ts:391, :747 and :953; the DEFAULT_CONFIG models only consignorAddress (ched-p-workflows.ts:62, :149, :250) with no search-term/selected-trader field; the a11y check is ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:128; and grep for consignor/Consignor across tests/notification/ched-p/ returns zero behavioural assertions. No new field or control was discoverable from the page object beyond the two rendered search inputs and the create-new link — the search/select branch remains a rendered-but-never-driven surface, so nothing was added to fields[].

exposes: linkCreateNewConsignorOrExporter (getByRole('link', { name: 'Create a new consignor or' }))
pageObject.note: The page object exposes ONLY the create-new link — it names no locator for the Name input, the Address input, the Search button, the results table, or the per-row View/Select buttons. Corroborates the 'Create a new consignor or exporter' secondary action (confidence inferred from tests; already confirmed by trace) and firms up that the search-and-select path is a rendered-but-never-driven surface.

workflowUsage: workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:390-391 — after tradersAddresses.linkAddConsignorOrExporter, the journey lands here and immediately clicks linkCreateNewConsignorOrExporter; the Name/Address filters and any Select action are skipped entirely.
workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:747 and :953 — same create-new-only pattern in the other two CHED-P workflow variants.

Consequence: DEFAULT_CONFIG has no field for a 'search term' or 'selected existing trader' — the config models only consignorAddress (the create path). The search/select branch is variant-only and untested.
assertions: tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:127-129 — checkPageAccessibility(page, 'Search Existing Consignor Or Exporter Page') runs axe against this page in journey order before clicking create-new. This is a stated requirement: the page must pass automated WCAG (axe) checks. confidence: inferred (asserted by test, not a rendered trace observation).

No ched-p .spec.ts asserts any behaviour, message, empty-state, or result-row content for this page — grep of tests/notification/ched-p/ for 'consignor' returns nothing. The search results, View action, Select action, and zero-match state are asserted nowhere.

openQuestions: The evidence-pointer URL pattern in the brief said '/consignor/search'; the trace (action 85, navigated-to) confirms the real path is '/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/traders/consignor/search' — note the '/traders/' segment. There was NO '?page=1' query string on the observed navigation (a prior version of this spec asserted one; it is not evidenced by the trace and has been removed). The 'Create a new consignor or exporter' link, however, points at '/traders/consignee/new?reimport=true' — consignee, not consignor — suggesting IPAFFS re-uses one trader-search/create page for both consignor and consignee roles, parameterised by role in the path.

The two-row sample implies a bounded result set that fit one page; no pagination control (Next/Previous or govuk-pagination) was rendered in this snapshot, and no page query param was present on the URL. Whether server-side pagination and a pagination component appear for larger result sets is a pure gap — never exercised in this trace.
No validation was observed on this page. Behaviour when the search returns zero matches (empty-state copy), or when Search is pressed with both filters blank, is a gap — not exercised in this trace.

The results are existing address-book / trade-partner traders. Reference data (the list of selectable traders) is user/account-scoped, not a fixed enum; the new app will source it from the trader/address-book service rather than hardcode it.

In this trace the user did not use the search filters or Select an existing trader — they clicked 'Create a new consignor or exporter' directly, so no observedValues were captured for the Name/Address inputs.
